title: Physician Assistant / Nurse Practitioner
summary: We are currently seeking a Physician Assistant or Nurse Practitioner to join our committed team of professionals. The Physician Assistant/Nurse Practitioner position is an essential outpatient clinical role, providing direct care to new and established pulmonary and/or sleep patients. It is necessary that within this role, the Advance Practice Provider cultivates and supports a strong team culture and collaborative environment centered on consistently delivering exceptional patient care. This position will have the peer support of 5 additional mid-levels that are currently working in the outpatient practice.
responsibilities:
Patient Care:
- Patients on home O2 or nocturnal CPAP/BIPAP/NIV who need clinic evaluation to continue current care.
- Preoperative visits for those patients with non-complex Pulmonary issues and upcoming surgical needs.
- Hospital follow-up of patients admitted with COPD exacerbation and other Pulmonary disease.
- Pulmonary rehabilitation follow-up.
- Telephone consultation and follow-up as needed for patient clinical questions unable to be answered by MA or other clinic staff.
- Management and continuity of care for patients with straightforward Pulmonary or Sleep-focused clinical complaints.
- Urgent clinic visits (with close support of Pulmonary physician) of potentially both new and established patients as needed.
- Provide high-quality patient care while collaborating with the research team to support and manage critical clinical trials.

Patient and Staff Education:
- Single or group patient education about inhaler therapy.
- Single or group patient education on disease processes such as COPD, etc.
- Establishing and maintaining educational forms and handouts for patients.
- Ongoing clinic staff education, as needed.
requirements:
- Completion of an accredited Physician Assistant or Nurse Practitioner program.
- NCCPA or AANP certified, respectively.
- Minimum 5-years total post training experience, minimum 2-years pulmonary experience required.
- DEA license eligible upon hire, per Colorado Drug Enforcement Administration's Advanced Practice Provider requirements.
- Willingness to travel to various clinic sites, as workflow dictates.
- Enthusiasm for working in an outpatient setting with a large team of physicians and clinic staff.
- Positive attitude, exemplary patient care, and willingness to adapt to changes in job description as needed for patient and clinic needs.
- Excellent verbal and written communication skills with patients and families, as well as clinic physicians and staff.
- Proficient with Office 365 software, and the ability to quickly learn and adapt to Electronic Medical Records.
physicalRequirements:
- Ability to safely and successfully perform the essential job functions consistent with the ADA, FMLA and other federal, state and local standards.
- Ability to drive between clinic locations for meetings.
- Ability to sit or stand for extended time periods.
- Must be able to lift and carry up to 30 lbs.
compensation:
- Competitive salary.
- Paid malpractice premiums.
- Benefits effective date-of-hire (no waiting period).
- Comprehensive medical benefits. Company covers 80-85% of individual or family premium.
- Dental and vision benefits offered at no cost to the employee. Company covers 100% of individual or family premium.
- Life and AD&D benefits.
- Optional additional life, disability and accident coverages at group discounts.
- PTO and holiday pay.
- 401k benefits with company contribution after eligibility period.
- CME stipend and allotted time.
- No call, nights, weekends, or holidays
